sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: SAP SE: Release of a capital market information
feed.summary: SAP SE announced details of its ongoing share buy-back program. In the period from March 30, 2026, to April 1, 2026, a total of 2,346,968 shares were repurchased via XETRA, with an aggregated volume of €344,949,638. The total number of shares bought back under the program until April 1, 2026, reached 16,280,097 shares.

feed.title: Did you buy this toothpaste? You may qualify for a cash payout.
feed.summary: Consumers who purchased Tom's of Maine toothpaste between November 21, 2020, and March 6, 2026, may be eligible for a cash payout from a class-action settlement. The lawsuit accused the company of deceptive marketing practices, which Tom's of Maine denies but has agreed to settle to avoid litigation costs. Claims must be filed by July 6, and payments will be distributed after a final court approval set for September 10.

feed.title: Coming Soon: Spring California Climate Credit to Deliver Energy Bill Savings to PG&E Customers
feed.summary: Millions of PG&E residential and small business customers will receive a California Climate Credit on their April energy bills, with residential customers getting $46.26 for natural gas and eligible small businesses receiving an electric credit of $36.18. The residential electric credit has been paused until later in the year, likely August and September, to coincide with higher-usage months, a decision by the CPUC following legislative direction to help manage energy bills. This credit is part of the state's Cap-and-Invest Program, aiming to cut carbon emissions while providing bill relief to customers.

feed.title: Oracle lays off over 500 Silicon Valley workers as California cuts top 700
feed.summary: Oracle has laid off 710 California employees, including 318 in Redwood City and 184 in Santa Clara, with the cuts effective by June 1. This adds to thousands of reported global layoffs for the tech giant, which previously moved its headquarters out of California. The layoffs come as unemployment rates rise in the Bay Area and other tech companies also reduce their workforces, despite Oracle reporting strong earnings recently.

feed.title: Arista Networks Gets Upgraded. AI Hyperscalers Could Push the Stock to a Record.
feed.summary: Arista Networks stock rose on Tuesday following a bullish upgrade from Rosenblatt Securities. The firm believes Arista's strong ties with major AI hyperscalers are poised for significant returns in the near future. The company currently trades down 3.6% on the year.

feed.title: Exxon Mobil Corp. stock: Strong returns amid energy shifts – what investors need to know
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. has shown strong financial performance with 5-year total returns of 244% and significant shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks. The company maintains resilience through low break-even costs, strategic investments in projects like Guyana's Stabroek block, and diversification into LNG and helium. Analysts and institutional investors show confidence, with a generally supportive stance and increased stakes, despite risks like oil price volatility and regulatory changes.

feed.title: Tech Moves: Microsoft leader jumps to Anthropic; Tagboard gets new CEO; Expedia names tech VP
feed.summary: Eric Boyd, a long-time Microsoft executive, has joined Anthropic to lead its infrastructure team after nearly 17 years at Microsoft. Additionally, Nathan Peterson is stepping down as CEO of Tagboard, a Redmond-based company, and will be succeeded by Marty Roberts, who previously served as the company's chief operating officer. Ryan Desjardins has also been promoted to vice president of technology at Expedia Group, contributing to the company's focus on AI capabilities.

feed.title: Target brings back Car Seat Trade-In program for 10th year
feed.summary: Target is bringing back its Car Seat Trade-In program for the 10th year, offering shoppers a 20% Target Circle Bonus for trading in old car seats. This year, the bonus can be applied not just to new car seats or baby gear, but also to toys, food and beverage, and apparel. The program has recycled over 58 million pounds of materials, preventing them from ending up in landfills.

feed.title: A pizza driver's detour went viral — and strangers sent him $100,000 in tips
feed.summary: A Domino's delivery driver named Dan Simpson made a quick detour to buy a customer a Diet Coke when his store was out, an act of kindness caught on video. The customer, Brian Wilson, who is visually impaired, shared the video, which went viral and led to a GoFundMe campaign raising over $100,000 for Simpson's retirement. Simpson, 68, plans to use the unexpected funds for travel, grateful for the overwhelming support after years of struggling to save.

feed.title: Rail Service Restored After Coloma Washout Disrupts CSX Freight, Amtrak Line
feed.summary: Rail service between Chicago and Grand Rapids is returning to normal after emergency repairs near Coloma due to a weekend washout. CSX crews swiftly repaired the flood-damaged track, reopening the line on Monday for freight service. Amtrak's Pere Marquette line is expected to resume service soon, though some delays may still occur as operations normalize.

feed.title: Seacoast Bank appoints Atlanta-based M&A expert to board of directors
feed.summary: Seacoast Bank, one of Florida's largest community banks, has appointed an Atlanta-based M&A expert to its board of directors. The bank, which expanded into the Atlanta market three years ago, plans to continue its growth in the region. The move highlights Seacoast Bank's strategic focus on the Atlanta market.

feed.title: Why Biogen (BIIB) could beat earnings estimates again
feed.summary: The article is a placeholder with "MSN" as its content, indicating that the actual content for "Why Biogen (BIIB) could beat earnings estimates again" was not loaded. Therefore, no meaningful summary can be provided.

feed.title: Caterpillar Stock Has Nearly Tripled From Its 2025 Low. Can the Rally Last With a $2.6B Tariff Bill Due?
feed.summary: Caterpillar (CAT) stock has seen a significant rally, nearly tripling from its 2025 low, driven by a strong Q4 2025 performance and a record $51 billion backlog. However, investors are concerned about a projected $2.6 billion tariff hit in 2026, which impacted Q4 operating profit. The company's valuation, especially its Power & Energy division, is trading at a premium compared to peers, and its Q1 2026 earnings report on April 23 will be crucial in determining if the rally can continue amidst tariff pressures.

feed.title: Arista Networks Is Down 28% From Its Peak. It May Finally Be Time to Buy ANET Stock
feed.summary: Arista Networks (ANET) has seen its stock drop 28.33% from its peak, presenting a potential buying opportunity despite trading at 35.83x forward earnings. The company recently reported strong Q4 2025 results, beating consensus and raising its 2026 AI networking revenue guidance, projecting 25% overall revenue growth. While memory price increases and potential tariffs pose risks, analysts suggest Arista is well-positioned in the AI infrastructure buildout, with a TIKR model target price of $309.53 by 2030.

feed.title: Humana Stock Pops 11% As CMS Finalizes 2027 Medicare Advantage Rates
feed.summary: Humana's stock surged 11% after the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) finalized a 2.48% rate increase for Medicare Advantage plans in 2027, exceeding initial proposals. This news boosted the entire managed care sector and is projected to funnel over $13 billion in additional payments. While Humana still faces challenges like margin pressure and integrating new members, the favorable rate update removes significant uncertainty for investors and provides breathing room for the company to work towards its 2028 earnings targets.

feed.title: Final Trades: Netflix, Hershey, Morgan Stanley, Pepsi and Alphabet
feed.summary: The article features a "Final Trades" segment from the "Halftime Report," where the Investment Committee shares their top stock recommendations for the second half of the year. The stocks highlighted in this segment are Netflix (NFLX), Hershey (HSY), Morgan Stanley (MS), Pepsi (PEP), and Alphabet (GOOGL). The content focuses on these specific stock picks and the segment's aim to guide investors.

feed.title: Alphabet stock price forecast: $301.67–$305.85 range in focus as GOOGL holds steady
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) is currently trading at $302.62, showing a daily gain of 0.91%. The stock is exhibiting short-term bullish momentum, though it faces intermediate resistance, with projections suggesting consolidation within the $301.67–$305.85 range. Regulatory challenges, tariff policies, and competitive threats from AI rivals like OpenAI are increasing uncertainty for Alphabet's global operations and core businesses.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of uniQure N.V. Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit on behalf of investors in uniQure N.V. (NASDAQ: QURE) who purchased securities between September 24, 2025, and October 31, 2025. This action follows a significant drop in uniQure's stock price after the company disclosed that the FDA no longer agreed that data from its Phase I/II studies for AMT-130 would be sufficient for a Biologics License Application submission. Investors have until April 13, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: SP Funds S&P Global Technology ETF (SPTE) Stock Price, News, Quote & History
feed.summary: This Yahoo Finance page provides detailed information on the SP Funds S&P Global Technology ETF (SPTE), including its current stock price, trading history, and performance. The ETF focuses on global large-cap information technology equities adhering to Shariah investment guidelines. It also lists key financial metrics, sector weightings, and related technology and Shariah-compliant ETFs.

feed.title: 12 Industrials Stocks Moving In Tuesday's Intraday Session
feed.summary: This article details 12 industrial stocks experiencing significant price movements during Tuesday's intraday trading session. It highlights both gainers, such as Huachen AI Parking Management and Megan Holdings, and losers, including ClearSign Technologies and Axon Enterprise, providing their respective percentage changes and market capitalization. The movements are attributed to various factors including recent earnings reports for some companies.

feed.title: Compass Diversified (NYSE:CODI) Given New $10.50 Price Target at B. Riley Financial
feed.summary: B. Riley Financial has raised its price target for Compass Diversified (NYSE:CODI) to $10.50 from $8.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, which implies a potential 7.8% upside. This adjustment comes after the company reported a significant earnings miss in its last quarter, with shares currently trading near $9.74. Despite the mixed analyst ratings, which average out to a "Hold" with a target price of $12.75, insider Stephen Keller recently purchased 30,000 shares, and institutional ownership stands at approximately 72.7%.

feed.title: UBS Sticks to Their Buy Rating for Conocophillips (COP)
feed.summary: UBS analyst Josh Silverstein maintained a Buy rating on Conocophillips (COP) with a $155.00 price target. This comes despite a recorded sentiment shift among corporate insiders towards selling, with the Chairman & CEO recently selling over 113,000 shares. Conocophillips reported Q4 earnings with revenue of $13.31 billion and a net profit of $1.44 billion, showing a decrease from the previous year's figures.

feed.title: Did you buy this toothpaste? You may qualify for a cash payout.
feed.summary: Consumers who purchased Tom's of Maine toothpaste products between November 21, 2020, and March 6, 2026, may be eligible for a cash payout from a class-action settlement. The lawsuit accused the company of deceptive practices, which Tom's of Maine denies. Claims must be filed by July 6, and a final approval hearing for the settlement is scheduled for September 10.

feed.title: Will Strength in Commercial Aerospace Drive Howmet's Growth?
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (HWM) is experiencing significant growth driven primarily by strong demand in the commercial aerospace market, which accounted for 53% of its fourth-quarter 2025 sales. The company's Engine Products segment saw a 19.7% revenue increase due to increased air travel and demand for new, more fuel-efficient aircraft and spare parts. While commercial aerospace is the main driver, the defense sector also shows positive momentum with increased demand for engine spares for legacy fighters.

feed.title: From Olay to Tampax, P&G brings more of its brands to the WNBA
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble (P&G) has announced a multi-year, multi-brand partnership with the Women’s National Basketball Association (WNBA), expanding its presence in women's sports. Building on Mielle’s existing partnership, brands like Secret, Olay, and Tampax will now engage with the league, leveraging its growing fanbase and cultural influence through various activations. The collaboration aims to celebrate WNBA athletes, amplify their stories, and drive retail growth for P&G brands.

feed.title: Millions of PG&E customers get automatic April bill credits
feed.summary: PG&E will automatically apply the Spring California Climate Credit to customers' April bills. Residential natural gas households will receive a $46.26 credit, while eligible small business customers will receive an electric credit of $36.18. This initiative is part of the state's Cap-and-Invest Program, which has cumulatively delivered nearly $1,200 to PG&E households since 2014, and aims to provide bill relief and promote cleaner energy solutions.

feed.title: Billions Flow Out of BlackRock's India ETF as Iran War Escalates
feed.summary: Investors are rapidly withdrawing funds from the largest US-listed ETF tracking Indian equities, BlackRock's iShares MSCI India ETF, due to escalating concerns about the global energy crisis and the impact of the Middle East conflict on India's economy. The ETF experienced over $220 million in withdrawals in a single day and more than $2 billion over five weeks. This outflow is driven by India's high reliance on fuel transiting the Strait of Hormuz and has led to a significant drop in the NSE Nifty 50 Index and a weakening rupee.

feed.title: BlackRock (NYSE: HPQ) holds 99.5M shares, 10.9% stake reported
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed an amended Schedule 13G/A, disclosing beneficial ownership of 99,527,329 shares of HP Inc (NYSE: HPQ) common stock, representing a 10.9% stake as of March 31, 2026. This filing, signed on April 7, 2026, indicates BlackRock has 92,816,267 shares with sole voting power and full dispositive power over all reported shares. The disclosure clarifies institutional ownership without signaling activist intent or immediate changes in voting or disposition plans.

feed.title: Bank of America Corp stock: Analyst buys signal strength amid steady growth outlook
feed.summary: Analysts are issuing "Buy" ratings for Bank of America Corp (BAC) stock, driven by its robust net interest income growth, strategic expansions, and resilience in a higher-rate environment. The bank's diversified business model and strong financial performance, including significant revenue and profit, position it as a compelling investment despite existing risks. Global investors can find value in BAC's scale, consistent dividends, and potential for earnings growth, making it a strong candidate for diversified portfolios.

feed.title: UP Fiber snaps up AT&T's wireline assets across Michigan’s Upper Peninsula and Mackinaw City
feed.summary: UP Fiber has acquired AT&T's wireline assets across Michigan’s Upper Peninsula and Mackinaw City, including over 9,000 miles of network infrastructure and 40 central offices. The financial terms of the deal were not disclosed. UP Fiber plans to invest significant capital to modernize and expand its network, partnering with Michigan Broadband to enhance communication services for over 200,000 locations.

feed.title: Coca‑Cola Marks America’s 250th Anniversary With Nationwide Celebration and Community Initiatives
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is marking America's 250th Anniversary with a nationwide celebration and community initiatives. Key programs include "America's Block Party," aiming to be the largest synchronized Fourth of July celebration in U.S. history, and "America Gives," which encourages volunteer service. The initiatives are part of a broader effort by America250 to unite the nation and celebrate significant moments between July 1 and July 5, 2026.

feed.title: Popular discount retailer opening new store in Henrietta
feed.summary: Ross Dress for Less is expanding its presence in the Rochester, NY area with a new store opening in Henrietta, next to Target, and another underway in Irondequoit. The Henrietta location involves a $1.2 million renovation of a former Babies R Us, while the Irondequoit store takes over a former Big Lots space. These additions will bring the total number of Ross stores in the greater Rochester market to four.

feed.title: Veteran Loan Fund launches Accelerator to boost Veteran and Military Spouse businesses
feed.summary: The Veteran Loan Fund (VLF), supported by Truist Foundation, has launched the Veteran and Military Spouse Business Accelerator to enhance economic opportunities for military-connected entrepreneurs. This initiative aims to provide capital, training, and business support, including deploying $15 million in loans and up to $1.5 million in grants over the next year. The 8-week program offers comprehensive business education, coaching, and access to funding for successful participants.

feed.title: MSCI Inc. Acquires PM Insights
feed.summary: MSCI Inc. (NYSE: MSCI) has acquired PM Insights, a specialist private markets data and analytics firm. This acquisition is set to enhance MSCI’s capabilities in private asset investing by providing institutional-grade data, pricing transparency, and analytics. PM Insights offers daily secondary market reference data, including pricing, valuation, transaction, and liquidity information for private company securities.

feed.title: RBC Digital Notes linked to Russell 2000 (OTC: RBMCF) with 17% digital payout
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada is offering Digital Notes linked to the Russell 2000® Index, providing a contingent fixed return of at least 17% if the index value at maturity is greater than or equal to its initial value. Investors face principal at risk if the index declines. The Notes do not pay interest, are subject to the issuer's credit risk, and are not listed on any securities exchange.

feed.title: A Dividend King on Sale: Is Abbott Labs a Healthcare Bargain?
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories (ABT) recently hit its 52-week low, prompting questions about its value as a healthcare bargain. While the Nutrition segment faces temporary headwinds, the Medical Devices division demonstrates strong, accelerating growth with market-leading innovations. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating, suggesting the stock is undervalued and offers a compelling opportunity for long-term investors due to its reliable dividend and strategic acquisitions.

feed.title: New veteran startup program targets $15M in loans and 1,500 jobs
feed.summary: The Veteran Loan Fund (VLF) has launched a new Accelerator program aiming to deploy $15 million in loans and up to $1.5 million in grants to 300 Veteran and Military Spouse entrepreneurs within 12 months, supporting 1,500 jobs. This initiative is backed by a Truist Foundation grant to PeopleFund and builds on VLF's prior success of having raised $30 million and facilitating over $110 million in business loans to 1,500 entrepreneurs since 2021. The program includes an 8-week instruction period, business coaching, financial training, and ongoing technical assistance for graduates.

feed.title: Rockwell Automation advances New Berlin mega-plant development
feed.summary: Rockwell Automation is moving forward with its mega-plant development in New Berlin, which will incorporate advanced automation, robotics, and digital technologies. This move is intended to demonstrate Rockwell's leadership in modern industrial operations. The project highlights the company's commitment to cutting-edge manufacturing practices.

feed.title: BlackRock (BLK) discloses 10.7% holding in iShares S&P 500 BuyWrite ETF (CUSIP 46438G711)
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed a Schedule 13G disclosing a 10.7% beneficial ownership stake in the iShares S&P 500 BuyWrite ETF, amounting to 601,530 shares. The filing indicates that BlackRock's reporting business units hold sole voting and dispositive power over these shares. It also notes that the iShares S&P 500 Premium Income ETF holds an interest exceeding 5% in the same common stock.

feed.title: BlackRock (KREF) holds 5.03M shares, 7.8% reported (Schedule 13G/A)
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed a Schedule 13G/A, reporting beneficial ownership of 5,026,321 shares of KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. common stock, which represents 7.8% of the class. The filing indicates BlackRock holds sole voting power over 4,928,841 shares and sole dispositive power over all 5,026,321 shares. This amendment to a passive investment disclosure was signed on April 7, 2026.

feed.title: BlackRock (BLK) discloses 11.5% ownership of iShares MSCI India ETF
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. (BLK) has filed a Schedule 13G/A disclosing an 11.5% beneficial ownership stake in the iShares MSCI India ETF, totaling 16,855,457 shares. The filing indicates BlackRock holds sole voting power over 16,341,829 shares and sole dispositive power over all 16,855,457 shares. The report also notes that the MSCI Emerging Markets ex China IMI Index Fund holds more than 5% of the ETF's common stock.

feed.title: Royal Bank of Canada (OTC: RBMCF) sells autocallable notes, 3‑yr Apr 2029 maturity
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada (OTC: RBMCF) is offering 3-year autocallable contingent yield notes with quarterly contingent coupons ranging from 9.0% to 9.7% per annum. These notes are linked to the performance of the Russell 2000® and S&P 500® indices, with payments dependent on both indices meeting specific coupon and downside thresholds. Investors face significant risks including potential principal loss, no guaranteed coupon payments, and limited liquidity due to the unlisted nature of the notes.

feed.title: BlackRock (LW) holds 11.8% of Lamb Weston, 16.35M shares reported
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed an amended Schedule 13G/A, reporting a beneficial ownership of 16,348,630 shares of Lamb Weston Holdings Inc. common stock, which represents 11.8% of the class as of March 31, 2026. The filing indicates that BlackRock's reporting business units hold significant voting and dispositive authority over these shares, and also notes that the iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF has an interest greater than five percent. This disclosure provides insights into institutional ownership and control without signaling any specific corporate actions or transactions.

feed.title: BlackRock (NYSE: BLK) discloses 21.96M shares (10.1%) of T. Rowe Price (TROW)
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has disclosed a 10.1% beneficial ownership stake in T. Rowe Price Group, Inc. (TROW), comprising 21,957,917 shares as of March 31, 2026. This Schedule 13G filing indicates a passive investment rather than an attempt to influence control of the company, with BlackRock holding sole voting power for 20,494,650 shares and sole dispositive power for all beneficially owned shares. The disclosure emphasizes the routine nature of such institutional filings for transparency in public markets.

feed.title: Barclays Issues Positive Forecast for Ryman Hospitality Properties (NYSE:RHP) Stock Price
feed.summary: Barclays has increased its price target for Ryman Hospitality Properties (NYSE:RHP) to $110 from $109, maintaining an "Overweight" rating and suggesting an 18.27% upside. This aligns with a generally positive analyst outlook, with ten analysts recommending a "Buy" and two a "Hold," resulting in a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a target of $111.27. The article highlights the company's current trading price, market capitalization, and significant institutional ownership, along with recent analyst actions from other firms.

feed.title: BlackRock (PAYC) holds 5.76M shares, 10.6% stake reported in 13G
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed a Schedule 13G, disclosing a beneficial ownership of 5,763,274 shares of Paycom Software, Inc. (PAYC) common stock, which represents a 10.6% stake as of March 31, 2026. The filing indicates BlackRock holds sole voting power over 5,612,689 shares and sole dispositive power over all 5,763,274 shares. The report also notes that iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF holds an interest exceeding five percent in Paycom common stock.

feed.title: BlackRock (NASDAQ: MTCH) reports 27.66M shares, 11.9% ownership
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed an amendment (Schedule 13G/A) reporting its beneficial ownership in Match Group, Inc. (MTCH). The filing indicates BlackRock beneficially owns 27,660,359 shares, which represents 11.9% of Match Group's outstanding common stock. This update shows BlackRock holds significant sole voting and dispositive power over these shares, with iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF identified as a holder with over 5% interest.

feed.title: BlackRock (MOH) discloses 6.12M shares—11.7% stake in Molina Healthcare (MOH)
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed an amendment to its Schedule 13G/A, disclosing a beneficial ownership of 6,116,371 shares, representing an 11.7% stake in Molina Healthcare Inc. as of March 31, 2026. The filing indicates sole voting power over 5,949,590 shares and sole dispositive power over all 6,116,371 shares, clarifying that these holdings aggregate across specific BlackRock business units. This passive investment suggests a portfolio or index-related interest rather than an activist stance.

feed.title: Coca-Cola ousts Pepsi as major hotel chain’s beverage provider
authors: Tim Darnell and Gray News staff
feed.summary: The Coca-Cola Company has secured a significant deal with Marriott International, replacing Pepsi as the hotel chain's global beverage provider. This agreement will place Coke products in Marriott's approximately 9,700 properties across 143 countries, representing a major victory in the long-standing rivalry between the two beverage giants. The deal is expected to boost Coca-Cola's core business through increased fountain pours, minibar sales, and catering volume, while also offering substantial marketing benefits.

feed.title: Regencell Bioscience (NASDAQ:RGC) Stock Price Down 8.9% - What's Next?
feed.summary: Regencell Bioscience (NASDAQ:RGC) shares fell 8.9% during mid-day trading, reaching $28.10 with significantly lower trading volume. Analysts maintain a "Sell" consensus rating for the company, while institutional ownership remains very low despite some new positions taken by hedge funds. The company specializes in Traditional Chinese Medicine for neurocognitive disorders.
